The issue, which includes significant articles, pays tribute to Anne Manton, a legendary figure in emergency nursing who recently passed away.

news.headline: Dickson introduces TotalCare with 24/7 Global Assist for environmental monitoring support, ensuring swift responses anytime, anywhere.
news.summary: Global assistance for environmental monitoring is now available around the clock with 24/7 support services. Located in Addison, Illinois, this service was launched on March 20, 2025. It offers rapid assistance to tackle temperature and humidity monitoring problems promptly, ensuring critical issues are addressed without delay. This service enables businesses to minimize disruptions and maintain proper environmental conditions at all times.

news.headline: BofA appoints Jason Edelmann as President of Fort Lauderdale market, aiming to enhance connections and drive growth.
news.summary: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida on March 20, 2025, Bank of America announced the appointment of Jason Edelmann as the president of Bank of America Fort Lauderdale. In his new role, Edelmann will oversee operations and serve as the primary leader for the market. His responsibilities include facilitating connections between clients, team members, and local communities to maximize the bank's offerings. This move is part of Bank of America's strategy to enhance its presence in Fort Lauderdale by leveraging Edelmann's leadership skills and experience.

news.headline: PCCA appoints Gus Bassani, PharmD, as CEO of its U.S. business unit, effective April 1, 2025.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, in Houston, PCCA, a top supplier of active pharmaceutical ingredients and consulting for compounding industry, revealed that Gus Bassani, PharmD, will take over as CEO of its U.S. division starting April 1. PCCA is registered with the FDA and provides essential ingredients and services for compounding.

news.headline: Quantum Rings integrates quantum simulation with NVIDIA CUDA-Q, enhancing GPU-accelerated quantum circuit simulation.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, in San Jose, California, Quantum Rings revealed their latest development in quantum simulation technology by merging it with NVIDIA CUDA-Q. This new integration offers GPU-accelerated quantum circuit simulation to a global audience of researchers, developers, and businesses. The collaboration aims to enhance the efficiency and speed of quantum computing tasks.

news.headline: Education workers in Calgary and Sturgeon accept settlements, ending strikes. CUPE 520 votes 93% in favor, CUPE 4625 votes 80% for settlement. Return to work: Monday at Calgary Catholic, March 31 at Sturgeon.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, two additional CUPE locals recently approved tentative agreements, leading to the conclusion of strikes at the Calgary Catholic and Sturgeon School Divisions. Members of CUPE 520 at the Calgary Catholic School Division supported their settlement with an overwhelming 93% vote in favor. Likewise, CUPE 4625 members at the Sturgeon School Division approved their agreement with an 80% majority. The return to work is slated for Monday at Calgary Catholic schools and March 31 at Sturgeon schools. Currently, only one group of education support workers remains on strike.

news.summary: Don Francisco's Coffee, a prominent premium coffee brand, has introduced its exclusive limited-edition flavor, Chocolate Raspberry, accessible solely online for a brief period. Launched on March 20, 2025, this new flavor is the most recent addition to the company's product lineup.

news.headline: Bull Shark card game live on Kickstarter, created by Haminations for chaotic bluffing fun. YouTuber's second campaign.
news.summary: In Los Angeles on March 20, 2025, Bull Shark, an uproarious card game created by Bryson from Haminations and Wombo Combo Games, is now available on Kickstarter for support. With a fun and unpredictable gameplay, players can bluff, trick, and steal their way to success, but must avoid getting caught. The game is the brainchild of popular Youtuber Haminations and marks his second Kickstarter campaign.

news.headline: ISCDD honors Sharon Mates, PhD, and Steven Paul, MD with leadership award at 23rd Annual Meeting in Las Vegas.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, in Las Vegas, the International Society for CNS Drug Development (ISCDD) made an announcement via PRNewswire. The ISCDD is a non-profit organization focused on advancing central nervous system drug development. They stated that Sharon Mates, PhD, and Steven Paul, MD, have been appointed to key positions.

Steer Health introduces SteerConnect, an AI-driven platform to simplify patient care access, enhancing efficiency.
news.summary: A cutting-edge platform has been introduced by Steer Health, a prominent AI-powered healthcare solutions provider based in Dallas on March 20, 2025. This innovative platform aims to eradicate the inefficiency of constant scrolling and the frustration of dead-ends in healthcare services. With smart triage, personalized care matching, and immediate information on provider availability, the platform revolutionizes the way individuals access healthcare. By leveraging artificial intelligence, Steer Health's platform ensures efficient and tailored care solutions to users.

news.headline: Douglas B. Price recognized as Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence for Estate Planning and Probate Law.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, in Tempe, Arizona, Douglas B. Price, Attorney, was recognized as a Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence in The Inner Circle for his notable work in Estate Planning and Probate Law. This recognition highlights Price's expertise in the field.

Barnes has been recognized as a Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence in The Inner Circle for his significant contributions to Industrial Safety Helmet Production. Barnes, a prominent figure in the industry, has been acknowledged for his achievements in this sector.

news.headline: ZachXBT reveals $20M profits by the 50x Hyperliquid whale, identified as British hacker William Parker. Source links past fraud cases.
news.summary: A British hacker named William Parker, previously known as Alistair Packover, was identified by Onchain sleuth ZachXBT as the mysterious whale who profited $20 million from highly leveraged trades on Hyperliquid and GMX. Parker had been arrested for stealing $1 million from two casinos in 2023 and had a history of hacking and gambling. The whale trader caused a $4 million loss in Hyperliquid's liquidity pool by intentionally liquidating an approximately $200 million Ether long position. The whale trader's wallet addresses were associated with past phishing schemes, and they took another multimillion-long position on Chainlink. Hyperliquid revised its collateral rules to prevent such incidents in the future. The whale trader utilized leverage of up to 50x on decentralized perpetual exchanges. Cointelegraph has not independently verified ZachXBT’s claims.

news.headline: UK fraudster identified as high-risk Bitcoin whale, linked to $20M in profits from crypto trades. Previous history includes $1M casino theft and fraud charges. Raised concerns over use of stolen funds in DeFi.
news.summary: An in-depth investigation by on-chain analyst ZachXBT has connected the Hyperliquid whale, a trader who made profits amounting to $20 million through high-leverage cryptocurrency trades, to a British online scammer. This individual, identified as William Parker, previously known as Alistair Packover, had been arrested in 2023 for stealing around $1 million from two Finnish casinos and had faced fraud charges in the UK in the 2010s. The results of the investigation were disclosed in a series of posts on X on Thursday, revealing that the trader's wallet activities reportedly included funds obtained through phishing scams and exploiting casinos. These findings have sparked concerns regarding the utilization of stolen funds in decentralized finance. For more details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

Keystone leases 4,679 sq ft to Madis Coffee Roasters at The Curtis in Philadelphia, set to open on March 21, 2025.
news.summary: A 4,679-square-foot space at The Curtis in Philadelphia's Independence Mall has been leased by Keystone to Madis Coffee Roasters of University City. The lease agreement is long-term and the new location is set to open on March 21, 2025. Madis, renowned for its precision in coffee roasting and community connections, selected The Curtis as their second location. This space will provide a unique coffee experience showcasing the art of coffee roasting.

news.headline: BetZillion's 2025 Sports Betting Demographics Research reveals key trends from 2023-2024, showcasing global betting behaviors.
news.summary: In Santa Venera, Malta, on March 20, 2025, BetZillion, a reputable provider of sports betting analysis, unveiled its findings from the 2025 Sports Betting Demographics Research. This comprehensive report delves into the betting habits of individuals globally, focusing on trends observed between 2023 and 2024. The study reveals significant insights into the behavior of bettors worldwide, offering valuable information for the industry. The data from the study captures the preferences and patterns of a substantial number of individuals engaged in sports betting, shedding light on the evolving landscape of this sector.

news.headline: New Solana Meme Coin Launchpad Pump.fun introduces PumpSwap Exchange to compete with Raydium, aiming to enhance token migration.
news.summary: Pump.fun has recently introduced its decentralized exchange to compete with Raydium. The platform aims to enhance the token migration process for users.

news.headline: JPMorgan analyst upgrades Spire Inc. (NYSE: SR) from Neutral to Overweight, raises price forecast to $85; cites favorable Missouri legislation and rate case developments as growth drivers.
news.summary: Analyst Richard Sunderland from JPMorgan increased the rating for Spire Inc. (NYSE:SR) from Neutral to Overweight, along with raising the price target from $72 to $85. Sunderland believes that Spire has the potential to boost its earnings and bridge its valuation gap due to regulatory improvements in Missouri and the SB4 legislation. Additionally, favorable winter weather was mentioned as a contributing factor in reducing 2025 guidance risks. The analyst provided further positive reasons for the bullish perspective.

news.headline: HHS and FDA launch Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool for foods under Secretary Kennedy with online searchable feature.
news.summary: In Silver Spring, Maryland on March 20, 2025, a new online searchable tool called the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool (CCT Tool) was introduced by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. The initiative was led by Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. from the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services. This tool aims to provide transparency regarding chemical contaminants and their levels in various consumer products. Its launch signifies a step towards greater public awareness and information access, with potential implications for health and safety.

Washington Trust Bancorp, Inc. declares 56 cents per share quarterly dividend, payable on April 11, 2025.

(NASDAQ: WASH) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.56 per share for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025. Shareholders of record on a specified date will receive the dividend on April 11, 2025.

news.headline: TCL Team Boxing League partners with Merit TV™ for global media distribution of first team-based professional boxing league.
news.summary: Dr. Phil McGraw's network will be the sole distributor of the TCL Team Boxing League, the first professional boxing league to have a team-based ownership structure. The league is set to be broadcasted across various platforms. This announcement was made on March 20, 2025, in New York.

Alpenglow Biosciences debuts LUMI imaging interface at ABRF 2025, integrates smart microscopy into light-sheet workflow.
news.summary: In Seattle, Alpenglow Biosciences, a prominent player in 3D spatial biology, has introduced LUMI, a user-friendly interface that seamlessly incorporates smart microscopy into the light-sheet workflow. LUMI, short for Light-Sheet User Microscope Interface, represents the latest advancement for Alpenglow's 3D i™ Hybrid Open-Top Light-Sheet (HOTLS) micro. The unveiling of LUMI's capabilities will be exclusive to participants at the Association of Biomolecular Resource Facilities (ABRF) 2025 Annual Meeting.

ETH ETF by BlackRock seen as ‘successful’ but ‘less perfect’ without staking, which accounts for 25% of Ether's supply. Staking yield ranges from 2-7% annually, holding a total of $7 billion with $2.5bn inflow and $358m outflow in the past 11 days. Joseph Lubin discusses Ethereum narratives at Digital Asset Summit.
news.summary: BlackRock's head of digital assets, Robbie Mitchnick, praised the success of the firm's Ether ETF but noted a limitation without staking, a key investment-generating feature. Mitchnick highlighted the challenges of implementing staking for Ether ETFs, noting potential benefits if resolved. Ethereum's staking deposits reached $85 billion by February 2024, accounting for 25% of circulating supply with an annual yield rate of 2-7%. However, staking comes with risks like slashing for misconduct. Ethereum co-founder Joseph Lubin discussed Ethereum narratives, suggesting focusing on practical applications for users and businesses. BlackRock pitches Ethereum to investors as a bet on blockchain adoption, innovation, tokenization, stablecoin adoption, and DeFi. ETH ETFs hold $7 billion with $2.5 billion inflow and $358 million outflow in the past 11 days, amidst market struggles.

news.headline: BoC's Gov. Macklem: I was not trying to convey the message that there is a higher chance of an unscheduled rate announcement.
Macerich and PenFed Credit Union sign exclusive sponsorship agreement for Tysons Corner Center Plaza, highlighting PenFed's community commitment.
news.summary: A new agreement highlights PenFed Credit Union’s continued dedication to the local community and its members. The agreement emphasizes PenFed's long-standing commitment to serving its members and the community.

news.headline: $31 million awarded in Ulster County for rear-end collision victim due to a distracted truck driver's negligence.
news.summary: In a significant ruling in Ulster County, New York on March 18, 2025, a jury granted $31 million in compensation to a woman who suffered severe injuries in a rear-end crash initiated by a distracted truck driver. This decision stands as a notable triumph for personal injury litigation, showcasing the importance of accountability in such cases.

news.headline: Williams-Sonoma faces profit pressure in 2025 despite strong brand, with revenue growing 8% to $2.46 billion. Analysts maintain a Neutral rating with a lowered price forecast of $170.
news.summary: After reporting fourth-quarter results, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) saw analysts adjust their price forecast. Revenue grew by 8% year over year to $2.46 billion, surpassing the expected $2.35 billion. Additionally, adjusted earnings per share were $3.28, higher than the anticipated $2.93. Looking ahead to 2025, net revenue for Williams-Sonoma is forecasted to fluctuate between -1.5% and +1.5%, with comparable sales ranging from flat to +3.0%, and an operating margin of 17.4% to 17.8%. This includes a 20bps impact from the prior year's extra week. Wedbush analyst Seth Basham, maintaining a Neutral rating, decreased the price target from $190 to $170. Basham expressed disappointment in the company's FY25 operating margin guidance due to tariff challenges. Investors were already expecting tariff pressures.

news.headline: ECI Software Solutions wins 3 Stevie Awards for sales and customer service. Marlene Summers named Silver 2025 Stevie Award winner.
news.summary: Marlene Summers, the senior vice president of Global Customer Support at ECI, has been honored with a Silver 2025 Stevie Award for her exceptional leadership and successful operational strategy. In addition, ECI received two Silver Stevie Awards for their innovative customer service support and advancements in front-line technology. These accolades showcase the company's dedication to excellence and commitment to providing top-notch support to customers.

news.headline: Revive Environmental appoints Douglas Hendry as CTO for PFAS destruction through SCWO tech. Hendry has over a decade of experience in SCWO and will aid in scaling up commercial solutions to meet demand.
news.summary: In Columbus, Ohio, Revive Environmental Technology, a prominent player in PFAS destruction utilizing Supercritical Water Oxidation (SCWO), has appointed Douglas Hendry as their new Chief Technology Officer (CTO). With more than ten years of expertise in SCWO technology development, process engineering, and overseeing large-scale system implementation, Hendry's leadership will play a pivotal role as Revive expands its commercial SCWO solutions to cater to the increasing demand for authorized, full-scale PFAS treatment.
